specific_location: Roxborough Park area 6723 Big Horn Trail
description: Bird had been noted for 4 days by home owner (John Ealy), as he reported to us. Medium sized slender yellow to orange bird who was an obvious oriole, with black on face and chin, and black on back, making striking contast to yellowish orange body.  Two wing bars, with upper broader, and additional white streaking on wings. . Black tail.  Stronger and larger bill than would be expected in an Orchard Oriole.  Bird did not call. First seen when it flew into a large chamisa, where the bird stayed "hunkered in" for a few moments before flying to some evergreen trees.  It spontaneously perched up in one of the trees, where it was entirely visible.  We never saw it going to feeders in homeowners yard.
